title: Black Death Spread Inspired Optimization Algorithm: Mechanisms and Mathematical Modeling
author: Zhang, Jincheng
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: In 1347 AD, the Black Death spread rapidly across Europe, causing massive deaths and profound social, economic, and cultural impacts. Inspired by this historical event, this paper proposes a novel heuristic optimization algorithm that simulates the Black Death's spread, isolation, mortality, mutation, and immune adaptation mechanisms to achieve a balance between global search and local exploitation. The algorithm guides search direction through the "infection transmission" mechanism among individuals in a population, enhances global exploration through an "isolation mechanism," maintains population diversity through a "death and rebirth mechanism," introduces nonlinear perturbations through a "mutation mechanism" to escape local extremes, and introduces an "immune adaptation mechanism" to prevent premature convergence. This paper details the algorithm's mathematical model and mechanism design, providing a new solution strategy for complex optimization problems.

Inspired by this historical event, this paper proposes a novel heuristic optimization algorithm that simulates the Black Death's spread, isolation, mortality, mutation, and immune adaptation mechanisms to achieve a balance between global search and local exploitation. The algorithm guides search direction through the "infection transmission" mechanism among individuals in a population, enhances global exploration through an "isolation mechanism," maintains population diversity through a "death and rebirth mechanism," introduces nonlinear perturbations through a "mutation mechanism" to escape local extremes, and introduces an "immune adaptation mechanism" to prevent premature convergence. This paper details the algorithm's mathematical model and mechanism design, providing a new solution strategy for complex optimization problems.
